_readme: Eval-gated model-routing allowlist. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH, read live by the CLI enforcer (src/cli.ts) and the Python port (src/router.py). Every mode is named for its USE CASE, not its model, and should exist ONLY because a recorded eval against your real data cleared a strict quality bar. Do NOT hand-add a mode without a passing eval + an evidence entry (see GOVERNANCE.md). A mode that fails a re-eval gets moved to 'retired' with a dated reason, never silently deleted. The six modes below are REALISTIC EXAMPLES to show the shape — replace them with modes your own evals justify.
provider_pin_note: mode.provider (optional) pins OpenRouter provider routing so a PRODUCTION call runs on the same underlying endpoint/quantization the eval was scored on (closes eval->prod drift; see the Anthropic 'infrastructure-noise in agentic evals' finding). Shape: {"order":["deepinfra"],"allowFallbacks":false,"quantizations":["fp8"]}. null = unpinned (the router picks). Populate it from a served-provider value captured by an eval/regression run, then let regression assert it stays put.

extract-bulk.purpose: High-volume field extraction whose output feeds aggregate analytics.
extract-bulk.use_when: N > 50 rows AND output is counted/grouped/distributed downstream (not acted on per-row).
extract-bulk.do_not_use_when: An operator will read a single output row and make a decision from it. ~20% disagreement with the frontier anchor on judgment-laden fields (e.g. seniority on ambiguous titles).

filter-auto-reply.purpose: Binary autoresponder / out-of-office detection in an email reply pipeline.
filter-auto-reply.use_when: Single binary decision (auto vs not auto). High volume, low per-row stakes.
filter-auto-reply.do_not_use_when: Any multi-class classification, intent detection, or judgment task. On multi-class labeling this model produces literal pattern-matches that are semantically wrong.

lint-code.purpose: Pre-pass code linter — surfaces edge-case bugs a longer frontier review may bury.
lint-code.use_when: You are about to review code AND want a fast, cheap supplemental pass. Findings MUST be re-evaluated by the orchestrator before action.
lint-code.do_not_use_when: You need the primary architecture/security review. In evals the frontier anchor caught a SQL-injection vector in real code that this model missed entirely.

digest-longcontext.purpose: Entity-grounded structured digest over large multi-document context (transcripts / long docs).
digest-longcontext.use_when: Synthesizing many long documents into structured signal (named people, topics, objections) where entity accuracy matters. Large context window, far cheaper than the frontier anchor.
digest-longcontext.do_not_use_when: The synthesis drives a high-stakes irreversible decision with no human review — the frontier model is the floor there. For image/scanned-PDF input use a vision mode instead.

extract-accurate.purpose: Higher-accuracy bulk field extraction — the precision-leaning sibling of extract-bulk.
extract-accurate.use_when: Bulk extraction where per-field accuracy matters more than raw throughput and latency is not the constraint. Beats the throughput model on anchor-agreement.
extract-accurate.do_not_use_when: You need maximum throughput / lowest latency on a huge synchronous batch — use extract-bulk there. Also NOT for single-row decisions a human acts on directly.

extract-multimodal.purpose: Vision extraction/transcription from a page IMAGE or scanned/image PDF whose text layer is absent.
extract-multimodal.use_when: A scanned/image PDF or page image where a text extractor returns nothing and you need grounded content off the pixels (e.g. a roster, an agenda). Pass --image <path> (repeatable). Matched the frontier vision anchor on recall and name<->value pairing with zero hallucinated values, at a fraction of the cost.
extract-multimodal.do_not_use_when: The page is text-extractable (a text layer already exists — the text modes are cheaper). Also NOT for a per-row value acted on without a human verify.

free.reason: Routed to non-web models that hallucinate when asked about current events. Use the orchestrator directly.

generic-cheap.reason: A mode named for a model instead of a use case. Too generic; it invited misuse on single-row decisions. Replaced by the narrower extract-bulk.

chat.reason: The candidate hallucinated entities in ~30% of drafting outputs. Drafting stays with the orchestrator.

online.reason: Real-time web research stays with the orchestrator + a first-class web search tool. The plugin produced unverifiable claims in tests.
